qa_pairs.question: If an Academic Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) was uploaded within the last three months, is it mandatory to submit a new CCV when applying for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if an Academic PI's CCV on file was updated within the previous 90 days, submitting a new CCV (even if new publications were added) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application is optional. In this scenario, CCDB will display the latest CCV available on file, its submission date, and the PI's selection of publications previously enabled by resources.

qa_pairs.question: What steps must an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) follow to submit their Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application if their existing CCV on file is older than 90 days?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic PI's CCV on file is older than 90 days, they are required to submit a new CCV with their RAC application. The PI must go to the CCV website, make any necessary changes to the Alliance CCV template, and submit it. Then, they need to enter the new CCV confirmation code on CCDB and click 'Update CCV'.

qa_pairs.question: When an Academic Principal Investigator (PI) submits a new Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) that includes newly added publications, how are these new publications indicated, and what is the process to mark a new publication as not enabled by computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new CCV is uploaded that contains newly added publications (compared to the ones on file), CCDB will remember previously reported publications and mark only the recently added ones with a red border. To identify a new publication as not enabled by computational resources, the PI must click on it twice: the first click will indicate it was enabled (with a checkmark), and the second click will remove the checkmark, showing it was not enabled.
